overview.what: A robust producer/consumer architecture for handling heavy background tasks. It demonstrates how to offload time-consuming work (like image processing or email sending) to separate worker processes to keep the main API responsive.
overview.how_it_works: The system uses BullMQ on top of Redis. 'Producer' nodes add jobs to a named queue. 'Worker' nodes pull jobs from the queue, processing them according to priority. It includes built-in support for retries on failure and a Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) for permanently failed jobs.
what_you_learn: Configuring job retry and backoff strategies
Monitoring job status and DLQ management

files.description: Worker logic for processing background jobs.
API endpoint adding jobs to the queue.
